purpose: Documentation page for the posts area: LinkedIn Post: Introducing Synapse. It explains operational behavior, user workflow, data ownership, or architecture details that future agents use when updating Synapse docs or app behavior. Key content: Running one AI agent is straightforward. Running ten of them at the same time without them stepping on each other, losing context, or failing quietly? That's a different problem entirely. The file is 29 lines long and should be treated as a knowledge/control-plane source rather than application code.
gotchas: Documentation may describe behavior that has moved into Electron IPC/services or .claude skills; verify implementation before making broad user-facing claims.
patterns: Documentation pattern: H1 title, short introduction, then operational sections with headings, tables, examples, or path references for future maintainers.
conventions: Markdown headings use a single H1 followed by H2/H3 sections for navigable documentation.
